data.narrative:
Eikon Therapeutics is a Series B biotech company founded by Luke Lavis, Eric Betzig, Robert Tjian, and Xavier Darzacq, currently valued at $3.3B post-money following $1.1B+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including same-stage companies Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series B-to-next-round step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the $3.3B last post-money valuation, yielding $6.3B. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $800M, with a range of $600M to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed target revenue. The wide disparity between these two methods reflects the tension between Eikon's current valuation and peer benchmarks at identical stage and sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $680M (low) to $7.2B (high), with a median of $3.5B. The current $3.3B valuation sits near this median but substantially exceeds peer-median guidance. Key uncertainties include the extent to which founder pedigree and any undisclosed clinical progress justify premium to peer multiples, and whether the peer set adequately reflects Eikon's specific technology position within frontier biotech.
